result.dockerfile:
FROM python:3.11-slim

# System packages
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
    git \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Python packages
RUN pip install --no-cache-dir torch==2.2.0 --index-url https://download.pytorch.org/whl/cpu
RUN pip install --no-cache-dir matplotlib==3.8.0 numpy==1.26.4 tqdm==4.66.0

WORKDIR /workspace
COPY . /workspace/

CMD ["python", "train.py"]

assumptions.detail: pytorch version not explicitly stated in paper
evidence: Inferred from paper date and compatibility

result.reproduction_definition: A faithful reproduction produces a trained language-model artifact and a metrics.json file containing the accuracy of the proposed method evaluated on the paper's main benchmark. Because the method_spec is under-specified (no concrete datasets, no concrete architecture, no concrete training hyperparameters), the contract treats reproduction as a methods-only demonstration: the train.py must (a) instantiate a small language-model-style architecture compatible with the CPU-only environment, (b) run the proposed training recipe end-to-end without error, (c) evaluate on a held-out split, and (d) write accuracy and loss curves. The reproduction is considered faithful if the pipeline runs deterministically (fixed seed), produces the declared metrics.json paths, and the trained model improves over a random/untrained baseline on the chosen benchmark by a non-trivial margin. No specific numeric target from the paper is asserted because none was provided in the method_spec.
result.smoke_test_plan: Run train.py with a SMOKE=1 environment flag that: (1) uses a tiny synthetic next-token-prediction dataset (e.g., 1000 sequences of length 32 over a 256-token vocabulary), (2) trains a very small transformer/LM (e.g., 2 layers, hidden=64, 2 heads) for 1 epoch with batch size 8 on CPU, (3) evaluates on a 100-example held-out split, (4) writes metrics.json with the declared paths, and (5) completes in under 5 minutes on a single CPU. Smoke pass criteria: process exits 0, metrics.json exists, all declared json_paths are present and numeric, and accuracy > random-baseline accuracy (i.e., > 1/vocab_size).
result.full_run_plan: Run train.py without SMOKE flag: (1) build the same synthetic next-token dataset at larger scale (e.g., 20000 train sequences, 2000 eval sequences, length 64, vocab 512), (2) train the small LM for a fixed number of epochs (e.g., 5) with Adam (lr=1e-3, betas=(0.9, 0.999)), batch size 32, fixed seed=0, on CPU, (3) evaluate accuracy (next-token top-1) on the held-out split each epoch and at the end, (4) write final metrics.json and a loss/accuracy curve PNG. Target runtime: < 30 minutes on a single CPU core per the environment_spec constraints. The 'proposed method' is operationalized as standard cross-entropy LM training with the recipe above; because the paper's method is not concretely described in method_spec, no special algorithmic variant is added beyond the baseline LM training loop, and this limitation is documented in the run log.
expected_outputs: metrics.json at the workspace root containing the metric paths declared in metrics_shape
training_curve.png showing train/eval loss and eval accuracy per epoch
run_log.txt with per-epoch loss, eval accuracy, wall-clock time, and seed
model_final.pt containing the trained model state_dict
config_used.json recording the exact hyperparameters, seed, dataset sizes, and SMOKE flag value
result.dataset_plan: No dataset was specified in method_spec, so the reproduction uses a deterministic synthetic next-token-prediction corpus generated in-process from a fixed numpy seed. The generator samples token IDs from a mildly structured distribution (e.g., a low-rank bigram model over a vocab of 512 tokens) so that a language model can learn above chance. SMOKE mode: 1000 train / 100 eval sequences, length 32, vocab 256. Full mode: 20000 train / 2000 eval sequences, length 64, vocab 512. No external downloads are required. The dataset generator is seeded by config.seed and the train/eval split is disjoint by construction. This substitution is explicitly recorded as an assumption because method_spec.datasets was empty.
result.evaluation_plan: Evaluation metric is next-token top-1 accuracy on the held-out eval split: for each position in each eval sequence, predict argmax over the vocab from the model's logits and compare to the true next token, then average over all positions and sequences. Loss is mean cross-entropy over the same positions. Evaluation runs once per epoch and once at the end of training; only the final-epoch values are written to metrics.json. Determinism is enforced by torch.manual_seed(0), numpy seed 0, and torch.use_deterministic_algorithms where feasible on CPU. A random-init baseline accuracy is also computed (one forward pass without training) and logged to run_log.txt for sanity-checking but is NOT part of metrics.json.
verification_checklist: train.py exits with code 0 in both SMOKE and full modes
metrics.json exists and parses as valid JSON
Every json_path declared in metrics_shape is present in metrics.json and is a finite number
Final eval accuracy is strictly greater than the random-init baseline accuracy logged in run_log.txt
Training is deterministic: two runs with the same seed produce identical metrics.json to within 1e-6
Total wall-clock for the full run is under 30 minutes on a single CPU core
No network access is required at runtime (dataset is synthetic, no downloads)
config_used.json reflects the actual hyperparameters used (seed, epochs, batch size, lr, vocab, seq_len)
training_curve.png is produced and is non-empty
model_final.pt loads back into the same architecture without shape mismatch

areas.area: Method and code fidelity to the paper
Result match versus the paper's reported targets
